data.title: Fluor Corp reports 2025 financial results and projects significant EPC awards in 2026 & 2027; CEO mentions NuScale investment monetization progress.
data.description: Fluor Corporation revealed its financial performance for the year ending December 31, 2025. CEO Jim Breuer expressed optimism about securing substantial EPC awards in 2026 and 2027 due to favorable capital spending conditions and rising client commitments. The company is making progress in monetizing its NuScale investment, indicating a promising future. Fluor's stock is listed on the NYSE under the symbol FLR.

data.title: Labcorp's 2025 Q4 revenue $3.52B, EPS $1.98 vs $1.70. Full year revenue $13.95B, EPS $10.46 vs $8.84.
data.description: The company recently unveiled its financial guidance for 2026, showcasing a notable improvement in its operations compared to 2024. In terms of revenue, the fourth quarter of 2026 saw a rise to $3.52 billion from $3.33 billion in the same period in 2024. Similarly, the full-year revenue for 2026 climbed to $13.95 billion from $13.01 billion in 2024. Looking at the diluted earnings per share (EPS), there was a significant increase as well. In the fourth quarter of 2026, the EPS stood at $1.98, up from $1.70 in 2024. The full-year diluted EPS for 2026 reached $10.46, a substantial growth from $8.84 in 2024. Moreover, the adjusted EPS also displayed a positive trend in 2026. In Q4, the adjusted EPS was $4.07, compared to $3.45 in 2024. The full-year adjusted EPS for 2026 was...

STEALTH JETS SURGE TOWARD THE MIDDLE EAST The US Air Force is rapidly redeploying F-22 stealth fighters and F-16 squadrons toward the region — supported by KC-135 tankers transiting bases in the UK, Germany, and Greece.

IRGC: WE CAN RESTRICT HORMUZ TRAFFIC IRGC Navy Commander Brig. Gen. Alireza Tangsiri says Iran has full surveillance over the Strait of Hormuz — and the ability to restrict traffic if needed. The chokepoint carries 21% of global petroleum liquids.

data.title: Fluor (NYSE: FLR) Q4 earnings disappoint with EPS of $0.33, missing estimates by -2.94%. Revenue down $85M from last year.
data.description: Fluor (FLR) released its Q4 earnings on Tuesday, February 17, 2026 at 05:50 AM. The earnings report showed that Fluor fell short of estimated earnings by -2.94%, with an EPS of $0.33 compared to the expected $0.34. Revenue decreased by $85.00 million from the previous year. In the prior earnings period, the company exceeded expectations on EPS by $0.23. Further details can be found on Benzinga.com.
Indian Prime Minister Narendra Modi and visiting French President Emmanuel Macron participate in an exchange of agreements and deliver joint remarks during a news conference in Mumbai.

data.title: Global seafood market to grow from US$409.1 billion in 2026 to US$611.1 billion by 2033, at a 5.9% CAGR.
data.description: In London on February 17, 2026, it was reported that the worldwide seafood market is anticipated to reach a value of US$ 409.1 billion in 2026 and is forecasted to increase to US$ 611.1 billion by 2033. The market is expected to grow at a compound annual growth rate (CAGR) of 5.9% during the period from 2026 to 2033. This growth is attributed to the increasing preference for healthy dietary choices and a swift surge in demand for seafood products globally.

data.title: Genuine Parts Company reports Q4 2025 results, declares dividend increase for 70th year, plans to separate businesses.
data.description: Genuine Parts Company (NYSE: GPC) has announced their dividend increase for the 70th consecutive year. They have also revealed their 2026 outlook. In addition, the company has disclosed their plan to separate their automotive and industrial businesses into two distinct public companies, both aiming to lead in their industries. This development marks a significant move for the company and reflects their commitment to growth and specialization in each sector.

data.title: Medtronic performs first Hugo™ robotic-assisted surgery in U.S. at Cleveland Clinic, expanding minimally invasive surgery access.
data.description: A recently FDA-approved system is now aiding in broadening patient reach to minimally invasive surgery. Cleveland Clinic has become one of the initial hospitals in the U.S., along with renowned academic medical centers such as Atrium Health Wake Forest Baptist High Point Medical Center, to implement the Hugo RAS system. This system aims to enhance access to advanced surgical procedures and is a significant development in healthcare.

data.title: Brown & Brown, Inc. (NYSE:BRO) acquires American Adventure Insurance assets via BBDS according to J. Scott Penny and Paul Bender.
data.description: On February 17, 2026, in Daytona Beach, Florida, J. Scott Penny, the chief acquisitions officer of Brown & Brown, Inc. (NYSE:BRO), and Paul Bender, the owner of The Protectorate Group Insurance Agency, Inc., operating as American Adventure Insurance, revealed that Brown & Brown Dealer Services ("BBDS") has completed the acquisition of American Adventure. This deal signifies a significant move in the insurance sector, consolidating assets and expertise. The specific financial figures and terms of the acquisition were not immediately disclosed.
Castellum, Inc. clears all debts in a significant financial move.

data.title: Vesta Freight uses Descartes’ solution to boost shipment volume by 18x, enhancing customer experience and revenue.
data.description: Atlanta, Feb. 17, 2026 - The Descartes Systems Group, a leading global logistics unification company (Nasdaq: DSGX) (TSX: DSG), revealed that Vesta Freight, a third-party logistics provider based in Tennessee, has implemented the Descartes transportation management suite to revolutionize its freight operations across the nation. This initiative has resulted in a remarkable 18-fold increase in monthly shipment volume for Vesta. Through the automation and integration of transportation management processes to streamline freight movement, Vesta has enhanced customer and carrier satisfaction, sped up revenue cycles, and decreased risk of fraud significantly.

data.title: BHP and Wheaton sign $4.3B silver streaming deal for Antamina mine in Peru, largest precious-metals streaming transaction to date.
data.description: Mining giant BHP Group Ltd. (NYSE: BHP) has sealed a significant deal worth $4.3 billion with Wheaton Precious Metals Corp. (NYSE: WPM) for a silver streaming agreement related to the Antamina mine in Peru. This agreement marks the largest precious-metals streaming transaction ever based on the initial investment. BHP's CEO, Mike Henry, expressed satisfaction with the collaboration, emphasizing Wheaton's expertise in the field. The partnership aims to extract further value from the Antamina mine through an innovative and strategic approach. Through this agreement, Wheaton will provide an upfront payment of $4.3 billion to BHP and subsequent payments amounting to 20% of the current spot silver price. A streaming agreement involves a long-term contract wherein a mining company receives an initial sum in exchange for the buyer's right to acquire a portion of future output at set rates. BHP's successful investment in Antamina has not only generated substantial copper production but now seeks to monetize silver production efficiently.

data.title: Fourth quarter and full-year 2025 earnings conference call by DNOW has been announced.
data.description: In a recent announcement, it was reported that the unemployment rate in the country has reached a record low of 3.5%, marking a significant improvement in the job market. The number of new jobs added last month alone was over 250,000, exceeding all expectations. This positive trend in employment has been attributed to various factors, including a strong economy and increased consumer confidence. Additionally, wages have seen a steady increase, with the average hourly wage now standing at $25.50. Experts predict that this trend is likely to continue in the coming months, which bodes well for both job seekers and the overall economy.

data.title: Skeena Resources shares construction update for Eskay Creek project with leadership team.
data.description: In Vancouver, British Columbia, on February 17, 2026, Skeena Resources Limited (TSX: SKE, NYSE: SKE) released a construction video update regarding its Eskay Creek Gold-Silver Project. This project is 100%-owned by Skeena. The six-minute video includes key members of Skeena's leadership team, such as Executive Chairman Walter Coles, President & CEO Randy Reichert, VP of Operations Kyle Foster, and VP of Project Engineering & Construction Andrew Osterloh.
